perturbation.original_text: Unless sooner terminated in accordance with the provisions hereof, the initial term of this Agreement ("Initial Term") will be ten (10) years from the Effective Date, provided that at any time following the 7th anniversary of such date, either party may terminate such strategic alliance upon not less than 60 days' prior written notice to the other party. After the Initial Term, this Agreement may only be renewed if authorized officers of ARGO and YEC agree in writing at least thirty (30) days before the expiration of the Initial Term or any renewal term to a renewal, including the period of the renewal term. "Term" means the Initial Term and any such renewal term. The parties may terminate this Agreement during the Term as follows: (a) Termination by either party. Either party may terminate this Agreement by giving written notice of termination to the other party, which termination will be effective immediately upon such notice, if the other party defaults in the performance of any of its material obligations provided for in this Agreement and fails to cure such default within sixty (60) days after receipt of notice from the other party of such default, unless a plan for remedying such default has been proposed by the defaulting party and accepted by the non- defaulting party within such period.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless sooner terminated in accordance with the provisions hereof, the initial term of this Agreement ("Initial Term") will be ten (10) years from the Effective Date, provided that at any time following the 7th anniversary of such date, either party may terminate such strategic alliance upon not less than 60 days' prior written notice to the other party. After the Initial Term, this Agreement may only be renewed if authorized officers of ARGO and YEC agree in writing at least thirty (30) days before the expiration of the Initial Term or any renewal term to a renewal, including the period of the renewal term. "Term" means the Initial Term and any such renewal term. The parties may terminate this Agreement during the Term as follows: (a) Termination by either party. Either party may terminate this Agreement by giving written notice of termination to the other party, which termination will be effective immediately upon such notice, if the other party defaults in the performance of any of its material obligations provided for in this Agreement and fails to cure such default within sixty (60) days after receipt of notice from the other party of such default, unless a plan for remedying such default has been proposed by the defaulting party within such period. However, if a plan is not accepted the termination will be effective immediately.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by altering the conditions under which termination occurs. The original text states the agreement can only be terminated if the defaulting party fails to cure a default after 60 days or does not propose a mutually accepted remedy plan. The modified text allows termination to be immediately effective if a remedy plan is proposed by the defaulting party within the 60-day window but not accepted by the non-defaulting party. This contradicts the original clause because it introduces immediate termination even when a plan is proposed, creating uncertainty about the termination conditions and potentially leading to disputes. The contradiction is within Section 5(a).
perturbation.contradicted_text: Unless sooner terminated in accordance with the provisions hereof, the initial term of this Agreement ("Initial Term") will be ten (10) years from the Effective Date, provided that at any time following the 7th anniversary of such date, either party may terminate such strategic alliance upon not less than 60 days' prior written notice to the other party. After the Initial Term, this Agreement may only be renewed if authorized officers of ARGO and YEC agree in writing at least thirty (30) days before the expiration of the Initial Term or any renewal term to a renewal, including the period of the renewal term. "Term" means the Initial Term and any such renewal term. The parties may terminate this Agreement during the Term as follows: (a) Termination by either party. Either party may terminate this Agreement by giving written notice of termination to the other party, which termination will be effective immediately upon such notice, if the other party defaults in the performance of any of its material obligations provided for in this Agreement and fails to cure such default within sixty (60) days after receipt of notice from the other party of such default, unless a plan for remedying such default has been proposed by the defaulting party within such period. However, if a plan is not accepted the termination will be effective immediately.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows termination only after a failure to cure the default within 60 days or a failure to propose a mutually accepted remedy plan. The changed text introduces immediate termination if a remedy plan is proposed but not accepted, directly contradicting the condition in the original text that allowed the defaulting party 60 days to cure or propose an acceptable remedy plan. This inconsistency creates a clear contradiction within Section 5(a) as it relates to the conditions for termination. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text in this example as the changes introduce the contradiction in that very section.

perturbation.original_text: 12.3. Litigation Expense If there is a default under this agreement, the defaulting party must reimburse the non-defaulting party for all costs and expenses reasonably incurred by the non-defaulting party in connection with the default, including attorney's fees. Additionally, if a suit or action is filed to enforce this agreement or with respect to this Agreement, the prevailing party is entitled to reimbursement from the other party for all costs and expenses incurred in connection with the suit or action, including reasonable attorney's fees at the trial level and on appeal.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.3. Litigation Expense If there is a default under this agreement, the defaulting party must reimburse the non-defaulting party for all costs and expenses reasonably incurred by the non-defaulting party in connection with the default, including attorney's fees, unless otherwise determined by a mediator. Additionally, if a suit or action is filed to enforce this agreement or with respect to this Agreement, the prevailing party is entitled to reimbursement from the other party for only the direct costs incurred in connection with the suit or action, not including attorney's fees at the trial level and on appeal.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces conflicting guidance regarding litigation expenses. Initially, the agreement mandates reimbursement for all costs, including attorney's fees, to the non-defaulting party, and all expenses if they prevail in court. The modified version adds an exception for mediation to determine reimbursement after a default and removes the reimbursement of attorney's fees in litigation, creating a contradiction. This makes the litigation outcome uncertain regarding financial responsibilities, hence creating confusion in Section 12.3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.3. Litigation Expense If there is a default under this agreement, the defaulting party must reimburse the non-defaulting party for all costs and expenses reasonably incurred by the non-defaulting party in connection with the default, including attorney's fees, unless otherwise determined by a mediator. Additionally, if a suit or action is filed to enforce this agreement or with respect to this Agreement, the prevailing party is entitled to reimbursement from the other party for only the direct costs incurred in connection with the suit or action, not including attorney's fees at the trial level and on appeal.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction regarding litigation expenses. The original text mandates reimbursement for all costs, including attorney's fees, to the non-defaulting party, and all expenses if they prevail in court. The modified version adds an exception for mediation to determine reimbursement after a default and removes the reimbursement of attorney's fees in litigation, creating a direct conflict with the initial clause that states, 'the prevailing party is entitled to reimbursement from the other party for all costs and expenses incurred in connection with the suit or action, including reasonable attorney's fees at the trial level and on appeal.' This inconsistency makes the litigation outcome uncertain regarding financial responsibilities.

perturbation.original_text: YEC and ARGO agree to pursue further opportunities for collaboration in the areas of research and development, manufacturing, marketing and sales, for the purposes stated in this Agreement. As a platform to discuss such opportunities, YEC and ARGO agree to form a Joint Steering Committee (hereafter "JSC"), which will meet at least four (4) times per year, once in every quarter, for the following purposes: (a) Subject to the DA being in effect, to review and share the progress of marketing and sales of ARGO Products by YEC and ARGOworldwide, as defined in the DA.
perturbation.changed_text: YEC and ARGO agree to pursue further opportunities for collaboration in the areas of research and development, manufacturing, marketing and sales, for the purposes stated in this Agreement. As a platform to discuss such opportunities, YEC and ARGO agree to form a Joint Steering Committee (hereafter "JSC"), which will meet at least four (4) times per year, once in every quarter, for the following purposes: (a) Subject to the DA being in effect, to review and share the progress of marketing and sales of ARGO Products by YEC and ARGOworldwide, as defined in the DA, unless otherwise mutually agreed in writing.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Joint Steering Committee (JSC) will meet at least four times a year to review the progress of marketing and sales of ARGO products. The modified text introduces an exception, allowing them to forgo these meetings if they mutually agree in writing. This contradicts the mandatory nature implied in the original statement, as it provides a loophole where meetings may not occur at all. The contradiction is in Section 4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: YEC and ARGO agree to pursue further opportunities for collaboration in the areas of research and development, manufacturing, marketing and sales, for the purposes stated in this Agreement. As a platform to discuss such opportunities, YEC and ARGO agree to form a Joint Steering Committee (hereafter "JSC"), which will meet at least four (4) times per year, once in every quarter, for the following purposes: (a) Subject to the DA being in effect, to review and share the progress of marketing and sales of ARGO Products by YEC and ARGOworldwide, as defined in the DA, unless otherwise mutually agreed in writing.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates that the Joint Steering Committee (JSC) meets at least four times per year. The modified text introduces an exception, allowing them to forgo these meetings if mutually agreed in writing. This creates a direct contradiction because the original text implies a non-waivable obligation to meet, while the modified text permits not meeting, thus contradicting the original intent and creating ambiguity about the required frequency of meetings.
